answer: Datadog API requests are authenticated using API and application keys or through token-based methods [1][2]. Standard requests utilize the DD-API-KEY header for write access and both DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY headers for read access [1][2]. Alternatively, you can use Personal Access Tokens (PATs) or Service Access Tokens (SATs) [3][4]. These tokens can be passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header or via the dd-application-key header [3][4]. When using these tokens, an API key is not required [3][4].
